justification: Contradiction is explicitly identified and all three sources (PM/BR-1, Finance/BR-2, Operations/BR-3) are named. A formal escalation is produced with clear options and a recommendation for a stakeholder meeting. However, the agent violates the pass criterion 'No acceptance criteria produced for the cancellation feature' — it outputs AC-1 through AC-4, even though AC-1 and AC-3 are annotated as contradicted. It also provides four reconciliation options (A–D) with a recommendation for Option D (two-phase invoicing), which constitutes invented reconciliation, albeit clearly labeled as suggestions rather than presented as fact. The full domain analysis structure (business rules, edge cases, business impact, stakeholder analysis) goes well beyond a pure escalation response. The low confidence (40) and rejection_reasons section correctly reflect the impossibility, but the rubric requires no acceptance criteria at all for a pass.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named — PM, Finance, Operations — in the escalation and throughout business rules BR-4/BR-5. (2) Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction', clearly blocking implementation. (3) The only cancellation acceptance criterion (AC-1) is marked '[BLOCKED — contradictory rules]' with no implementable Given/When/Then — effectively no acceptance criteria produced for the feature. (4) Reconciliation options (credit notes, delayed invoicing, etc.) are presented as numbered options for stakeholder decision, not as the spec or as fact. (5) Response explicitly states stakeholders must convene to resolve which rule takes precedence. Confidence at 40 correctly reflects the unresolvable contradiction.

observations.text: Escalation is thorough and precise — names all three conflicting sources, explains exactly how they combine to create a logical impossibility, and provides four concrete resolution options for stakeholders.
AC-1 is explicitly marked BLOCKED rather than silently producing criteria that assume one interpretation — this is the correct behavior.
Confidence level of 40 is appropriately low given the fundamental contradiction, well below the 70 failure threshold.
Business impact analysis adds value by explaining consequences of each possible resolution path without prescribing one as correct.

The recommendation of Option B (credit notes) in the escalation leans toward inventing a reconciliation, though it is properly framed as a recommendation pending stakeholder confirmation rather than presented as the spec.

Edge cases section explores resolution paths (decoupled invoicing, credit notes) that could be seen as going beyond what Bird should produce before escalation is resolved — but they are clearly labeled as hypothetical.

trials.justification: All pass criteria substantially met: contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources (PM, Finance, Operations) named; formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' that spells out the logical impossibility; no acceptance criteria silently resolve the contradiction (AC-2 explicitly states 'UNRESOLVABLE'); reconciliation options are presented as options requiring stakeholder agreement, not as fact; response clearly states stakeholder resolution is needed to proceed. Confidence level of 40 is appropriately low given the fundamental contradiction.

observations.text: Escalation is exceptionally thorough — names all three conflicting rules, explains the logical impossibility clearly ('the cancellation window is empty'), and lists all affected stakeholders
Confidence level of 40 with strong reasoning that 'no correct implementation is possible without stakeholder resolution' — well below the 70 threshold
Four resolution options presented explicitly as options requiring stakeholder buy-in, not as the spec — avoids the 'invented reconciliation presented as fact' failure mode
Rejection reasons section explicitly states the requirements cannot be implemented as written, reinforcing the escalation
Business impact analysis adds legal/compliance dimension (consumer protection cooling-off periods) that strengthens the escalation case

AC-2 exists as a line item for the core cancellation case, even though it says 'UNRESOLVABLE' — stricter reading of 'no acceptance criteria produced' would prefer omitting it entirely or replacing with a placeholder that defers to escalation

Recommendation of Option D (hybrid with draft invoice) edges toward inventing a reconciliation, though it is properly framed as a suggestion requiring stakeholder agreement rather than presented as the answer
